author: @howardsimpson489
text: Robert, I had a pre WW2 Singer sports car which had plain spur gears for the diff gears. These six gears  half overlapped and half engaged with the output spur gears. Their  six shafts were rotated as an assembly by the crown wheel. Very quiet and smooth when rotating just one rear wheel off the ground. Never seen one in any other vehicle.

author: @marcfruchtman9473
text:
Such a cool invention. Thank you for demonstrating this. 
One thing I was not sure about is, does the axle turn at the same speed when restricted vs unrestricted in the model you created?

author: @xt8ob
text: Another amazing video.  Since finding your channel, I've grown in both my Tinkercad designs and my understanding of building with gears and the like.  If I could make a request, I'd love to see a Tinkercad tutorial on making a 3D printed tellurian--just the Earth, Moon, and Sun.  Calculating the gears, making the Earth tilt, having the Moon rotate about the Earth--all of that would be rich in content and super helpful.  Thanks for creating such good content!

author: @WynterLegend
text: What I am swiftly coming to realize, is that Divinci could have built a whole damned car, if he only had a dedicated blacksmith, and an alchemist to make fuel.

replies.author: @alexprach
replies.text: The steam engine wasn't made yet which only needed wood as a fuel. I reckon he could probably make a spring powered human helicopter too, but again the cost to make such a large and unnecessary blacksmith probably didn't warrant it back then.

replies.author: @RUFUSTKO
replies.text:
Da Vinci did actually build a vehicle that was powered by a clockwork engine, but I know what you mean. It kind of blows my mind that he did not come up with a sterling engine (ie, glass tubes) considering Venice was the glass making capital of the world at the time. 

Jesus Christ Almighty God bless you all

author: @disklamer
text:
"Crude gets you up and running, and complex stops you."
I like to start any type of project with a proof of concept even if it is made from paperclips or paper maché, so I don't make a fool of myself due to some glaring oversight. If I can cobble together a working model out of the flimsiest materials, the "real" version will gold up just fine ;)

replies.author: @GabrielPettier
replies.text:
"A complex system that works is invariably found to have evolved from a simple system that worked. A complex system designed from scratch never works and cannot be patched up to make it work. You have to start over with a working simple system." – John Gall 

I like to keep that in mind.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @erhardpostinger1326
text:
1:42 "wenn nicht sogar noch weiter" passt sehr gut, denn Leonardos Zeit litt nicht unter der Fuchtel von Patenten, die den Fortschritt behindern. 
Will sagen, Leonardo konnte abkupfern so viel er wollte. 
Keine Meute von Patentanwälten biss sich in seine Wadln.

Bei allem Verständnis für die Notwendigkeit, dass Entwickler nicht um die Früchte ihrer Arbeit gebracht werden sollen, sollte der Fortschritt auch nicht behindert werden.

Eine Aufgabe für Politiker, um die ich sie nicht beneide.
